legal_standard: IT Act 2000 Section 65B + Trade Marks Act 1999
executive_summary: CINEOS identified 20 Amazon India and 20 Flipkart listings using explicit counterfeit keywords ('first copy', 'replica', 'duplicate', 'master copy') in product titles. All listings include explicit admission of counterfeit nature in the product title itself. Evidence is IT Act 65B certified.

evidence_standard: Each finding includes the product title containing explicit counterfeit admission, the product URL, SHA-256 hash of the evidence, and timestamp. Full findings list available on request.
what_cineos_can_prove: Product title contains explicit counterfeit keywords
URL is an active Amazon India / Flipkart listing
Evidence SHA-256 hashed at time of detection
Trade Marks Act 1999 Section 29 violation prima facie
what_cineos_cannot_prove: Seller legal identity (available in seller dashboard)
Whether seller is authorised (Amazon/Flipkart can verify)
requested_actions: Remove listings with explicit counterfeit keywords
Suspend seller accounts with multiple violations
Share seller details with brand legal teams
Refer to police for repeat offenders
cineos_offer: CINEOS scans Amazon India and Flipkart daily for counterfeit listings across 20 major brands. Brand protection subscription: Rs 10L/year per brand. Contact: yugandhar@cineos.in
